corpus.title: Hegseth orders review of U.S. troops deployed to NATO in fiery Brussels speech
corpus.signal_text:
Hegseth orders review of U.S. troops deployed to NATO in fiery Brussels speech

U.S. Secretary of War Pete Hegseth put NATO allies on notice Thursday, announcing a Pentagon review of America's military commitment to Europe and warning some countries will fail the assessment.

corpus.title: Hegseth puts NATO on notice as he launches review of US troops in Europe and blasts allies for 'shameful' behavior
corpus.signal_text:
- See more Daily Mail on Google - save us as a Preferred Source
Pete Hegseth lashed out at NATO allies on Thursday as he announced a six-month Pentagon review of American forces in Europe.
The US Defense Secretary said the outcome of the review will depend on how fast the Europeans take responsibility for their own security
The review was yet another surprise for European allies and Canada as they learn to deal with an increasingly unpredictable ally.
US officials and senior military officers had promised to coordinate closely with the Europeans as America draws down.
In recent months, President Donald Trump and the Pentagon have sent conflicting signals about whether America is reducing or increasing its military footprint in Europe, as well as threatening to annex Greenland, a semiautonomous island that is part of ally Denmark.
Just weeks ago, the Trump administration said that it would no longer provide as much military support should any NATO member come under attack.
'This will be a real review. It will be designed to ensure that NATO is moving fast and irreversibly toward Europe leading, stepping up to take primary responsibility for the defense of Europe,' Hegseth told his NATO counterparts.
'It´s a review that some countries will fail and others will pass with flying colors.'
United States Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, right, speaks during a meeting of the North Atlantic Council in defense ministers format at NATO headquarters in Brussels, on Thursday
NATO's Supreme Allied Commander Europe General Alexus Grynkewich, front right, and Chair of the NATO Military Committee Admiral Giuseppe Cavo Dragone, front left, listen to the speech
German Chancellor Friedrich Merz later said the allies have long been aware of US plans to pull troops from Europe at some point and that they must take care of their own security.
'We know that we must do more and we are doing it,' Merz said.
In a fiery speech at NATO headquarters in Brussels, Hegseth lambasted European allies for failing to provide US forces access to bases in Europe to launch attacks on Iran, calling it 'shameful.'
'These allies, they put America's sons and daughters, our sons and daughters, at risk by denying them the predictable access, basing and overflight that never should have been in question at all,' he said.
The review would also assess whether the US has full access and overflight 'when we need it.'
While defense ministers and military officers sat in silence, Hegseth railed against migrat

corpus.title: Trump aumenta la presión sobre los aliados europeos de la OTAN
corpus.signal_text:
Trump aumenta la presión sobre los aliados europeos de la OTAN

Durante una reunión de los ministros de Defensa de la OTAN en Bruselas, el secretario de Defensa de Estados Unidos, Pete Hegseth, criticó duramente a los socios europeos y amenazó con revisar la presencia de tropas estadounidenses en el continente. Desde el regreso del presidente estadounidense, Donald Trump, a la Casa Blanca, Washington ha arremetido contra sus aliados europeos, a quienes acusa de no destinar suficientes recursos a la defensa.

corpus.title: Hegseth Tells NATO US Will Review Force Presence in Europe
corpus.signal_text:
Hegseth Tells NATO US Will Review Force Presence in Europe

US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said the Pentagon will review troop deployments in Europe within six months and warned allies that US support and NATO funding contributions could be cut if they fail to boost defense spending, amid tensions over Europe’s response to the Iran war.

corpus.title: Amenaza EU con retener una parte de sus cuotas a la OTAN
corpus.signal_text:
Amenaza EU con retener una parte de sus cuotas a la OTAN

Bruselas. El secretario de Guerra de Estados Unidos, Pete Hegseth, anunció una nueva revisión del despliegue de tropas estadunidenses en Europa y amenazó con retener parte de las cuotas de Estados Unidos a la Organización del Tratado del Atlántico Norte (OTAN) si los aliados que se “aprovechan del sistema” no cumplen con sus compromisos de gasto en defensa.

corpus.title: US to withdraw forces from Europe; is this the beginning of Trump's 'pulling the plug' on NATO?
corpus.signal_text:
US to withdraw forces from Europe; is this the beginning of Trump's 'pulling the plug' on NATO?

The Pentagon is initiating a six-month review to reduce US troop presence in Europe, prompting allies to shoulder more responsibility. Fighter jets, drones, and warships earmarked for European crises will be scaled back, creating gaps NATO members must now fill. This shift emphasizes Europe's need to lead in defense.
